metadata.description: Small internal corpus for narrow JS/TS semantic merge and source-ledger admission.

broadSuiteGaps.reason: local checkout proof records skipped versus checked-out repositories without cloning, installing, or reading source text
npm, pnpm, and yarn command metadata is recorded offline; checked-out repository install/build execution remains opt-in
linked fixtures cover representative merge surfaces, not repository-wide TypeScript diagnostics or project references
each real repository still requires LICENSE verification before source can be cached outside this manifest

entries.shape: large TS modules with overloads, type-only imports, exported declarations, and dense control flow
plugin/config TS with import churn, object members, exported helpers, and route-like ordered maps
JS parser/printer modules with comments, import specifier churn, generated-boundary exclusions, and function edits
App-router TSX and routing modules with JSX child insertions, prop contracts, async component boundaries, and type/value import churn
component/runtime modules with JSX-style child ordering, stable props, comments, and export helpers

fixtures.title: independent import additions keep both imports
independent exported value declarations keep both exports
same exported name added by worker and head is unsafe
independent interface property additions keep both members
independent type alias property additions keep both members
independent object literal member additions keep both members
class method additions keep both members
TSX-ish JSX text edit survives coordinator-only prefix trivia
TSX child additions from distinct gaps merge under one JSX parent
React shell child insertions merge in distinct JSX gaps
overload implementation body edit survives coordinator-only prefix trivia

workerLines: export function parse(value: string | number): string {
headLines: export function parse(value: string | number): string {
expectedLines: export function parse(value: string | number): string {

fixtures.title: comments and directive trivia remain byte-preserved by source ledger
generated/source-map boundary remains review-required metadata
decorator metadata plus stale malformed source blocks auto-merge
member edge policy emits structured unsafe reasons
same object member edited by worker and head is unsafe
same function control-flow edited by worker and head is unsafe
divergent import.meta host-context edits stay blocked
worker delete plus head modify of same function is unsafe
safe import specifier and declaration additions are merged
type-only and value import dependency additions merge with exported type declarations
workerLines: import { type BaseModel, type WorkerShape, decode } from './schema.js';
headLines: import { type BaseModel, decode, encode } from './schema.js';
export function serialize(value: ApiModel) { return encode(value); }
expectedLines: import { type BaseModel, decode, encode, type WorkerShape } from './schema.js';
export function serialize(value: ApiModel) { return encode(value); }

fixtures.title: safe top-level and unordered member additions merge atomically
independent new import declarations merge with declarations
member conflict blocks composed top-level output
import specifier reordering is a semantic no-op when bindings are preserved
default binding additions to existing imports merge with named additions
Vite config import default and named additions merge on an existing declaration
required source-ledger spans block replay admission when missing
computed top-level object keys are unsafe parser-ledger anchors
same object member added by worker and head is unsafe
order-sensitive object regions cannot use unordered member merge
